title: Owner Duty, Stakeholder Outcomes, Privacy, and Evidence · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers owner duty, stakeholder outcomes, privacy, and evidence into a changed owner-approved site-quality mission charter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of owner-approved site-quality mission charter with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, page population, acceptable delay, success, failure harm, and observable value.
Record controlling owner, allowed product identity, origins, paths, methods, schedule, purpose, expiry, and revocation contact.
Separate robots preferences and technical access from contracts, privacy, copyright, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Stakeholder Outcome
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 4765 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate owner permission, stakeholder value, privacy minimization, legal routing, and evidence layers; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, page population, acceptable delay, success, failure harm, and observable value.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Stakeholder Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define stakeholder, affected users, decision, page population, acceptable delay, success, failure harm, and observable value. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Stakeholder Outcome, keep Stakeholder Outcome connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
